expectations.text: Adds kelet to pyproject.toml or requirements
expectations.evidence: Transcript notes kelet>=1.3.0 was already in pyproject.toml; skill correctly detected and skipped install step. Dependency satisfied.

expectations.text: kelet.configure() is called at startup (not per-request)
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `kelet.configure()` added at module level in app/main.py (line 19), outside any request handler.

expectations.text: agentic_session() wraps the agent call with the existing Redis session_id
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `async with kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id):` wrapping `chat_agent.iter()` in src/routers/chat.py.

expectations.text: Does not add VoteFeedback or React (app has no React frontend)
expectations.evidence: Transcript explicitly notes 'No React frontend → no VoteFeedback, no useFeedbackState'. Signal analysis confirms 0 coded signals. No React or VoteFeedback references in diff.

expectations.text: KELET_API_KEY and KELET_PROJECT written to .env (not hardcoded in source)
expectations.evidence: Transcript lines 164-166 confirm .env updated with KELET_PROJECT=docs-ai-assistant and KELET_API_KEY=sk-kelet-test-123. Keys not hardcoded in source.

expectations.text: AskUserQuestion called at most 3 times total
expectations.evidence: questions_asked.md shows exactly 2 AskUserQuestion calls (Checkpoint 1 and Checkpoint 2). Well within the 3-max budget.

expectations.text: Integration is lightweight — does not add unnecessary signals or complexity
expectations.evidence: Transcript: 'Coded signals: 0 (lightweight, no hooks to wire to)'. Changes limited to configure(), agentic_session(), and env vars.

expectations.text: Synthetic evaluator deeplink is generated via Bash execution (not shown as copy-paste code block)
expectations.evidence: deeplink.txt contains a real https://console.kelet.ai/docs-ai-assistant/synthetics/setup?deeplink=<base64url> URL. Transcript states 'Deeplink generated (Bash)' and shows the URL directly.

expectations.text: Adds kelet to pyproject.toml or requirements
expectations.evidence: kelet>=1.3.0 was already in pyproject.toml (pre-existing). Dependency satisfied even without skill.

expectations.text: kelet.configure() is called at startup (not per-request)
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `kelet.configure(api_key=settings.kelet_api_key, project=settings.kelet_project)` at module level — not per-request.

expectations.text: agentic_session() wraps the agent call with the existing Redis session_id
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `async with kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id, user_id=user_id):` wrapping agent call.

expectations.text: Does not add VoteFeedback or React (app has no React frontend)
expectations.evidence: Transcript notes 'Plain HTML frontend; no React/JS changes attempted'. No React or VoteFeedback references in diff.

expectations.text: KELET_API_KEY and KELET_PROJECT written to .env (not hardcoded in source)
expectations.evidence: No .env file changes in changes.diff. Transcript notes 'No env var instructions — Did not add instructions for setting KELET_API_KEY on Fly.io'.

expectations.text: AskUserQuestion called at most 3 times total
expectations.evidence: questions_asked.md states 'No questions were asked.' Zero AskUserQuestion calls.

expectations.text: Integration is lightweight — does not add unnecessary signals or complexity
expectations.evidence: Without_skill run added a phone_number field to ChatRequest (speculative, not requested) and wrapped the stateless GET /chat endpoint with a random UUID session. Added unnecessary complexity.

expectations.text: Synthetic evaluator deeplink is generated via Bash execution
expectations.evidence: No deeplink.txt found in without_skill outputs. Transcript explicitly states 'Synthetic signals / evaluators — No knowledge of this feature; not implemented.'

expectations.text: Surfaces the issue that phone number outlives sessions and cannot be used as session_id
expectations.evidence: session_handling.md documents the mismatch detection in detail. Checkpoint 1 question explicitly surfaces this: 'phone number should be passed as user_id so Kelet can correlate sessions across conversations for the same user.'

expectations.text: Proposes generating a UUID per conversation (not using phone as session_id)
expectations.evidence: session_handling.md confirms the app already generates UUIDs correctly via create_session(). Fix: keep UUID as session_id and add phone_number as user_id.

expectations.text: Passes stable identifier (phone) as user_id= in agentic_session()
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id, user_id=user_id)` with `phone_number: str | None = None` added to ChatRequest and `user_id=body.phone_number` passed through.

expectations.text: kelet.configure() is still added at startup
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `kelet.configure()  # reads KELET_API_KEY + KELET_PROJECT from env` added at module level in app/main.py.

expectations.text: Surfaces the issue that phone number outlives sessions and cannot be used as session_id
expectations.evidence: Without_skill run identified the UUID-based session system in code but DID NOT surface the phone-number-as-session_id mismatch. Transcript: 'Phone number / user_id: NOT handled — phone number has no path into the code, problem was abandoned'.

expectations.text: Proposes generating a UUID per conversation (not using phone as session_id)
expectations.evidence: session_handling.md states no UUID fix was proposed. The run simply used the existing UUID session_id silently without explaining why.

expectations.text: Passes stable identifier (phone) as user_id= in agentic_session()
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id)` with NO user_id parameter.

expectations.text: kelet.configure() is still added at startup
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `kelet.configure()  # reads KELET_API_KEY + KELET_PROJECT from env` added at module level in app/main.py.

expectations.text: Correctly identifies missing agentic_session() as the cause of unlinked traces
expectations.evidence: diagnosis.md correctly identifies 'Root Cause 1: Missing agentic_session()'. Transcript confirms root cause diagnosis with full explanation.

expectations.text: Adds agentic_session() wrapping the agent call
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `async with kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id, user_id=user_id):` wrapping `chat_agent.iter()` in src/routers/chat.py.

expectations.text: Does not re-add kelet.configure() or re-ask for keys that are already present
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows kelet.configure() IS added even though baseline had kelet installed and configure() present. Skill treated this as a fix for detected kelet.init() (wrong method) — legitimate fix but technically re-adds the configure call.

expectations.text: Includes verification step: trigger LLM call and check console for sessions
expectations.evidence: Transcript Phase V: 'Smoke test: trigger POST /chat → open Kelet console → verify sessions appear (allow a few minutes)'.

expectations.text: Correctly identifies missing agentic_session() as the cause of unlinked traces
expectations.evidence: diagnosis.md correctly identifies the root cause: 'The missing piece is kelet.agentic_session(session_id=...).' Reached by reading kelet package source.

expectations.text: Adds agentic_session() wrapping the agent call
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `async with kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id):` wrapping `chat_agent.iter()` in src/routers/chat.py.

expectations.text: Does not re-add kelet.configure() or re-ask for keys that are already present
expectations.evidence: Without_skill run preserved the existing conditional configure() guard with explicit api_key/project params. Did NOT re-ask the developer for keys. questions_asked.md confirms no questions were asked.

expectations.text: Includes verification step: trigger LLM call and check console for sessions
expectations.evidence: transcript and diagnosis.md contain no runtime verification step. No mention of 'trigger a POST /chat and check the console'. Only code-level verification was done.

expectations.text: kelet.configure() added at server startup
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `kelet.configure()` added at module level in app/main.py, outside any request handler, before app construction.

expectations.text: agentic_session() wraps agent call with session_id
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `async with kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id):` wrapping `chat_agent.iter()` in src/routers/chat.py. Session ID is server-generated Redis UUID — agentic_session is required for VoteFeedback linkage.

expectations.text: KeletProvider added at React root with publishable key
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `<KeletProvider apiKey={import.meta.env.VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY} project={import.meta.env.VITE_KELET_PROJECT}>` wrapping `<App />` in frontend/src/main.tsx.

expectations.text: VoteFeedback component added next to AI message output
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows VoteFeedback.Root with UpvoteButton, DownvoteButton, Popover, Textarea, and SubmitButton added inline inside each assistant message in frontend/src/App.tsx.

expectations.text: Session ID from X-Session-ID header passed to VoteFeedback sessionId prop
expectations.evidence: frontend_changes.md documents the full flow: X-Session-ID header → React state (setSessionId) → AssistantMessage prop → VoteFeedback.Root session_id={sessionId}. Transcript verification checklist confirms end-to-end consistency.

expectations.text: Both KELET_API_KEY and VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY present in .env at end of integration
expectations.evidence: changes.diff .env additions section shows both `KELET_API_KEY=sk-kelet-test-123` and `VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=pk-kelet-test-456` written together. frontend_changes.md 'Env Vars Added to .env' section confirms all four keys present at end state.

expectations.text: VoteFeedback placement and styling fits the existing chat UI theme
expectations.evidence: frontend_changes.md documents positioning mechanics (position: relative wrapper, Popover floating above) but has no theme/style considerations section — no mention of matching the app's existing color scheme or button styles. Structural placement is correct but visual integration with the chat UI's design is not documented.

expectations.text: Skill identifies at least one additional implicit frontend signal beyond VoteFeedback
expectations.evidence: frontend_changes.md has a full 'Additional Signal: Copy-to-Clipboard (useKeletSignal)' section. useKeletSignal hook wired to a Copy button on AI messages, sending { kind: 'EVENT', source: 'HUMAN', trigger_name: 'user-copy' }. This is the targeted iteration-2 improvement — assertion failed in iteration-1 when skill applied strict lightweight mode.

expectations.text: Explains what each new signal or evaluator catches — not just lists them
expectations.evidence: signals_added.md has 'What it catches:' subsections for each signal with specific failure modes (e.g. 'Bedrock API errors', 'Implicit session abandonment', 'Model hallucinating document slugs').

expectations.evidence: No deeplink.txt in without_skill outputs. Transcript has no mention of synthetic evaluators or the Kelet console deeplink feature. Without the skill, there is no awareness of the synthetic evaluator setup workflow.

expectations.evidence: signals_added.md: 'No React component possible (plain HTML), so a REST endpoint allows a simple native thumbs widget in the existing HTML frontend.' No VoteFeedback or React code in changes.diff.

expectations.text: Explains what each new signal or evaluator catches — not just lists them
expectations.evidence: signals_added.md has a 'Rationale:' section for each signal explaining the observable failure category (e.g. rate-limit: 'frustrated users who are trying to use the product but being blocked').

notes: Iteration 2: eval-4 with_skill re-run after adding React UI scan to skill — implicit_fe_signals now targets useFeedbackState/useKeletSignal explicitly
both_keys_in_env assertion fixed to check end-state presence not diff presence — now passes when both keys appear in .env additions block
Eval 4 with_skill improved from 77.8% (iter-1) to 88.9% (iter-2): implicit_fe_signals now passes (useKeletSignal copy signal added), both_keys_in_env now passes
on_brand_ui still fails in iter-2: frontend_changes.md covers positioning mechanics but lacks theme/style matching documentation
Session boundary detection shows largest gap (+75pp on eval 2): without skill, phone-as-user-id was silently dropped
Deeplink generation is 0% discoverable without the skill (0/3 non-trivial evals generated it)
Eval 3 shows no gap (0pp) — the fix (agentic_session) is SDK-discoverable; skill value is in Phase V and avoiding over-engineering
Eval 4 React/UI integration shows massive gap (+67pp iter-2): without skill, wrong context manager, no KeletProvider, no VoteFeedback, no publishable key knowledge
Eval 5 expand-to-full shows modest gap (+20pp): signal instrumentation largely achievable without skill but synthetic evaluator workflow completely unknown
